stanzas:
Eetti Kuiyyo Muiyyo Song Lyrics
Singers: KG Ranjith, Vandana Srinivasan
& Maalavika Sundar
Lyrics: Na Muthukumar

Oduthey oduthey nenjamum oduthey
Thannaley undhan pinnaley
Thaththudhey thaavudhey chellamai koovuthey
Thannaley undhan kannaley
Yenguthey thenguthey en uyir vaanguthey
Ellam unnaaley

Kuiyyo muiyyo kuzhandhai poley
Kaadhal vaalaatuthey
Aiyyo aiyyo undhan kangal
Yennai thaalaatuthey

Oduthey oduthey nenjamum oduthey
Thannaley undhan pinnaley
Thaththudhey thaavudhey chellamai koovuthey
Thannaley undhan kannaley

Bommai pol sirikuriye
Saavi naan tharalaama
Pathiyam kolludha de
Konjam naan thodalaama
Anjaaaru mutham kitham
Ippa koduthuka da
Panchaangam paatha pinne
Pancha pichika da
Ayyo oru dhinusa romba pudhusa
Nee parkayil verkuren de

Kuiyyo muiyyo kuzhandhai poley
Kaadhal vaalaatuthey
Aiyyo aiyyo undhan kangal
Yennai thaalaatuthey

Paarvayil konjuriye
Morachadhum kenjuriye
Pakisthan army pol
Border-a minchuriye
Kannalam aagure varaikum
Kaaval thaangavilla
Unnala thoongumbodhum
Aaval thoongavilla
Ayyo edhu kolusa en manasa
Unna paarthadhum sinungudha da

Oduthey oduthey nenjamum oduthey
Thannaley undhan pinnaley
Thaththudhey thaavudhey chellamai koovuthey
Thannaley undhan kannaley

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the theme of Kuiyyo Muiyyo?

The song is a lively and romantic number that captures the playful essence of young love.

Who are the singers of Kuiyyo Muiyyo?

The song is sung by K.G. Ranjith and Vandhana Srinivasan.

Who composed the music for Eetti?

The music for the movie Eetti was composed by GV Prakash Kumar.

What is the context of the song in the movie?

The song appears in a sequence that highlights the blossoming romance between the lead characters.

When was the movie Eetti released?

The movie Eetti was released in the year 2015.
enrichedMetadata.summary: The song Kuiyyo Muiyyo from the movie Eetti is a vibrant and romantic duet that showcases the playful dynamics of young love. Sung by K.G. Ranjith and Vandhana Srinivasan, the song is set to the energetic beats of GV Prakash Kumar's composition. The lyrics by Na Muthukumar add a poetic charm, making it a perfect fit for romantic and celebratory occasions. The song captures the joy and excitement of a budding romance, leaving listeners with a sense of happiness and enthusiasm.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Experience the vibrant energy of Kuiyyo Muiyyo from the movie Eetti, where GV Prakash Kumar's lively composition meets the dynamic vocals of K.G. Ranjith and Vandhana Srinivasan. This peppy duet captures the essence of youthful romance and joy. Dive into the rhythmic beats and let the music take you on a delightful journey.
stanzaMeanings: Running, running, my heart is running. By myself, behind you. Beating, jumping, calling out sweetly. By myself, through your eyes. Where is it going, where is it taking my life? It's all because of you.
Like a child, love is playing. Oh no, oh no, your eyes are making me sway.
Running, running, my heart is running. By myself, behind you. Beating, jumping, calling out sweetly. By myself, through your eyes.

You smile like a doll. Can I give you the key? Will you accept it? Can I touch you a little? A kiss is ready, give it now. After checking the calendar, give me a high five. Oh, one day feels so new. When you look at me, I sweat.
Like a child, love is playing. Oh no, oh no, your eyes are making me sway.

You tease with your look. You beg when you are angry. Like the Pakistan army, you cross the border. Until it becomes a mirror, I can't guard it. When you sleep, I can't sleep. Oh, what is this sound in my heart? When I see you, it trembles.

Running, running, my heart is running. By myself, behind you. Beating, jumping, calling out sweetly. By myself, through your eyes. Where is it going, where is it taking my life? It's all because of you.
Like a child, love is playing. Oh no, oh no, your eyes are making me sway.
